title: T-078 — Manufacturing Operation Edit modal (SET-056)
prompt:
# T-078 — Manufacturing Operation Edit modal (SET-056)

## Goal
Build SET-056 spec-driven from `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md` §8.9 / UX SET-056 plus the production contract notes below. The adjacent modal prototype `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/modals.jsx:535-572` is pattern-only reference for dialog density and primitives, not a 1:1 screen source.

Add/Edit form modal. Edit mode shows operation_name + process_suffix as read-only (per §8.9.4 Edit mode rules). Validation V-SET-MFG-01..05.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/components/settings/modals/manufacturing-operation-edit-modal.tsx [create]
- apps/web/components/settings/modals/manufacturing-operation-edit-modal.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given the production component renders, then it satisfies UX SET-056 Manufacturing Operation Edit Modal / PRD §8.9.4 as the source of truth. Adjacent prototype snippets may be reused only as pattern references for matching primitives/density; they must not override UX route, fields, states, validation, or Manufacturing Operations semantics.
- Given Edit mode opens for an existing op, when fields render, then operation_name and process_suffix are read-only text — not inputs.
- Given user enters process_suffix='M1@', when validated, then Zod rejects (uppercase alphanumeric only, V-SET-MFG-01).

## Test strategy (RED first)
1. Add a failing test that exercises the acceptance criteria above. The RED command is:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/components/settings/modals/manufacturing-operation-edit-modal.test.tsx [create]`
2. Run RED — it must fail before any production code is written.
3. Implement the smallest change needed for GREEN.
4. Re-run RED command — must pass.
5. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the changed package; both must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not edit files outside the listed scope without documenting why in CLOSEOUT.
- Do not weaken existing acceptance criteria from prior tasks.
- Do not introduce REST endpoints; use Server Actions per stack contract.
- Do not commit secrets or vault keys.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED→GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + Server Actions. Primitives in `packages/ui`. PRD: `docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md` v3.5.

pipeline_inputs.description: SET-056 Manufacturing Operation Edit Modal production component (spec-driven; no exact prototype).
pipeline_inputs.details: Spec-driven UI: no exact canonical prototype exists under `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/*.jsx` for SET-056 Manufacturing Operation Edit Modal. `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md` §8.9 and PRD §8.9.4 replace prototype parity as the authoritative contract. prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/modals.jsx:535-572 is adjacent/pattern-only reference for matching primitives, density, and interaction conventions; it is not a 1:1 source and must not change Manufacturing Operations fields, states, validation, route, or semantics. Production contract: data via Server Components (Drizzle); mutations via Server Actions; primitives from `packages/ui` only (no @radix-ui/react-dialog import outside packages/ui per §0).
acceptance_criteria: Given the production component renders, then it satisfies UX SET-056 Manufacturing Operation Edit Modal / PRD §8.9.4 as the source of truth. Adjacent prototype snippets may be reused only as pattern references for matching primitives/density; they must not override UX route, fields, states, validation, or Manufacturing Operations semantics.
Given Edit mode opens for an existing op, when fields render, then operation_name and process_suffix are read-only text — not inputs.
Given user enters process_suffix='M1@', when validated, then Zod rejects (uppercase alphanumeric only, V-SET-MFG-01).

UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: write RTL test that mounts the page with mocked data and asserts the parity AC items + ac_extra coverage.
GREEN: build component, wire Server Action(s).
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not import @radix-ui/react-dialog outside packages/ui (§0 ESLint rule).
Do not paste prototype JSX wholesale or treat adjacent prototypes as 1:1 sources — translate UX §8.9 to the production contract.
Do not regress accessibility (axe violations = fail).
Do not claim 1:1 prototype parity for this screen; UX §8.9 is authoritative.
